summary: Fowler explores the relationship between Model-Driven Software Development (MDSD) and Domain-Specific Languages (DSLs), noting that while DSLs are prominent in MDSD circles, they exist and serve similar purposes in traditional programming contexts. He explains that MDSD DSLs require specialized projectional editing tools and language workbenches, which creates pragmatic challenges due to less established tooling support. Fowler observes that DSLs receive proportionally more emphasis in the MDSD community, possibly because that community is smaller with less established practice. He remains skeptical of MDA-based DSLs specifically while acknowledging that model-oriented and textual DSLs share common foundations in semantic models.
key_points: DSLs serve the same role in both MDSD and traditional programming contexts, targeted at specific problems versus general-purpose languages
Model-oriented DSLs require projectional editing and language workbenches, which have less mature tooling ecosystems
MDSD DSLs can work independently within traditional language environments, generating code without requiring a general-purpose MDSD model
The MDSD community's emphasis on DSLs may reflect its smaller size and less established practices rather than superior sophistication
Both textual and model-oriented DSLs share the foundation of working with semantic models, though MDSD typically assumes code generation rather than direct execution
money_quotes: Some MDSD people seem to think that DSLs only exist within the MDSD world.
DSLs exist in both the textual language and MDSD worlds and play pretty much the same role for both.
In order to use model-oriented DSLs you need a different, projectional editing, approach to tooling.
Cynics think this is a result of the MDSD community desperately searching for a way to remain relevant, fans of MDSD regard it as a sign of MDSD's superior sophistication.
I think this is mainly due to the fact that the MDSD community is smaller and has far less in the form of established practice.
A difference is that most MDSD people assume that you'll want to generate code from that model rather than executing the model directly.
key_insight: DSLs are not exclusive to the MDSD world—they serve similar purposes in traditional programming, with the main differences being in tooling approaches and assumptions about code generation versus direct execution.
